claims.label: Definition (Pair lift)
claims.statement: Given two complex channels z_1[n]=a[n]+ib[n] and z_2[n]=c[n]+id[n], the pair lift is q[n]=z_1[n]+z_2[n]j = a[n]+b[n]i+c[n]j+d[n]k.
claims.notes: Defines the basic two-channel quaternion lane construction.

claims.label: Definition (Canonical grouped lift)
claims.statement: For ordered channels z_c[n], the canonical grouped lift forms lane g from channels (2g,2g+1) as Q[g,n]=z_{2g}[n]+z_{2g+1}[n]j when both exist, and uses Q[g,n]=z_{2g}[n] for an odd tail channel.
claims.notes: Fixes deterministic pairing and odd-channel handling.

claims.label: Proposition (Exact recoverability of ordered captures)
claims.statement: For every ordered complex-channel capture Z, unpack(lift(Z)) = Z, so the canonical grouped lift is injective and lossless at the representation layer.
claims.proof_sketch: Recover each full lane by reading quaternion coordinates into two complex channels; in the odd-tail case recover the single channel from scalar and i components.

claims.label: Proposition (Determinism of canonical grouped lift)
claims.statement: For fixed ordered input Z, the grouped lift lift(Z) is unique because channel pairing is fully specified by a fixed index rule and does not depend on optimization, statistics, or learned parameters.
claims.proof_sketch: The mapping from channel indices to lane indices is fixed and total.
claims.notes: Reproducibility guarantee under fixed channel ordering.

claims.label: Proposition (Energy additivity per full lane)
claims.proof_sketch: Expand q[n] into four real coordinates and compare squared norms term-by-term.
claims.notes: Per-lane invariant used for capture-wide preservation.

claims.label: Corollary (Capture-wide energy preservation)
claims.statement: Summed over all lanes and sample indices, the total lifted quaternion energy equals the original summed complex-channel energy, with zero contribution for a missing odd-tail partner channel.
claims.proof_sketch: Apply per-lane additivity and sum over all lanes and indices.
claims.notes: Global invariant under canonical grouped lifting.

claims.label: Theorem (Main representation theorem)
claims.statement: The canonical grouped lift from C^{C×N} to H^{G×N} defines a deterministic, lossless internal representation of ordered multichannel complex captures with exact recovery and capture-wide energy preservation.
claims.proof_sketch: Combine determinism, exact recoverability, and energy-preservation results established in prior propositions and corollary.
claims.notes: Core theorem of the paper's representation layer.
